revision.comment: 新しいページ：代田山狐塚古墳（長野県飯田市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 代田山狐塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[長野県]][[飯田市]]松尾代田1403-71[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/shitei-pref.html 県指定文化財]（飯田市ホームページ）。 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 34.49 |経度度 =137|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 7.58 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長42m |築造年代 = [[4世紀]]代 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = |史跡指定 = 長野県指定史跡「代田山狐塚古墳」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |ラベル = 代田山狐塚
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''代田山狐塚古墳'''（しろたやまきつねづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[飯田市]]松尾代田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 長野県南部の[[飯伊地域|下伊那地方]]において、[[天竜川]]右岸の中位段丘の東端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。現在までに墳丘を良好に残し、[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に測量調査が実施されているが、発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方に向ける史跡説明板。。急傾斜面上に位置するため、低地からの視線を受ける東側の方が高く見えるように墳丘が形成されている。墳丘北側・西側には周濠が存在したとされるが、そのほかに出土品等は知られていない{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。資料が乏しいため築造年代の特定はなされていないが、墳形から[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]代の築造と推定され、伊那谷では最古の古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。飯田地域では[[5世紀]]後半以後に[[ヤマト王権]]の影響下で[[前方後円墳]]を主とする[[飯田古墳群]]が営造されるが、その前時代である本古墳では前方後方墳が採用される点で性格を異にし、本古墳の場合には特に東海地方勢力とのつながりが想定されている{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 古墳域は[[1994年]]（平成6年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 * 墳丘長：42メートル（史跡説明板では44.5メートル） * 後方部 ** 幅：28メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：11.3メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：13メートル == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 代田山狐塚古墳 - 1994年（平成6年）2月17日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 代田山狐塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会・飯田市教育委員会、1997年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林正春|year=2002|chapter=狐塚古墳 > 代田山狐塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 代田山狐塚古墳の測量調査]|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 飯田における古墳の出現と展開]|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 飯田における古墳の出現と展開 -資料編-]|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[飯田古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 代田山狐塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 {{DEFAULTSORT:しろたやまきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:飯田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]]

古墳 |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''代田山狐塚古墳'''（しろたやまきつねづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[飯田市]]松尾代田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 長野県南部の[[南信州地域|下伊那地方]]において、[[天竜川]]右岸の中位段丘の東端部に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。現在までに墳丘を良好に残し、[[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）に測量調査が実施されているが、発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方に向ける史跡説明板。。急傾斜面上に位置するため、低地からの視線を受ける東側の方が高く見えるように墳丘が形成されている。墳丘北側・西側には周濠が存在したとされるが、そのほかに出土品等は知られていない{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。資料が乏しいため築造年代の特定はなされていないが、墳形から[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]代の築造と推定され、伊那谷では最古の古墳に位置づけられる{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。飯田地域では[[5世紀]]後半以後に[[ヤマト王権]]の影響下で[[前方後円墳]]を主とする[[飯田古墳群]]が営造されるが、その前時代である本古墳では前方後方墳が採用される点で性格を異にし、本古墳の場合には特に東海地方勢力とのつながりが想定されている{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 古墳域は[[1994年]]（平成6年）に長野県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 * 墳丘長：42メートル（史跡説明板では44.5メートル） * 後方部 ** 幅：28メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：11.3メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：13メートル == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 代田山狐塚古墳 - 1994年（平成6年）2月17日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 代田山狐塚古墳]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（長野県教育委員会・飯田市教育委員会、1997年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林正春|year=2002|chapter=狐塚古墳 > 代田山狐塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|代田山狐塚古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 代田山狐塚古墳の測量調査]|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 飯田における古墳の出現と展開]|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|title=[http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 飯田における古墳の出現と展開 -資料編-]|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[飯田古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 代田山狐塚古墳] - 公益財団法人 八十二文化財団 {{DEFAULTSORT:しろたやまきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:飯田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:長野県指定史跡]]

[[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]

[[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * **Mound length:** 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * **Rear section width:** 28 m * **Neck width:** 11.3 m * **Front section width:** 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site** - [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * **Mound length:** 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * **Rear section width:** 28 m * **Neck width:** 11.3 m * **Front section width:** 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site** - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * **Mound length:** 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * **Rear section width:** 28 m * **Neck width:** 11.3 m * **Front section width:** 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site** - [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

[[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

revision.text:
[[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} == [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} == Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | construction_period = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} shape, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' 11.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Cultural Properties == === Nagano Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' - Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Cultural Heritage Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Information Site "Bunka Iida").. == References ==
