Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | artifacts = | designation = Prefectural other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designated as a Nagano Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial artifacts have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designated a Nagano Prefectural other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == other_designation == === Nagano Prefectural other_designation === * '''other_designation''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | designation = Prefectural other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | designation = Prefectural other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | label_position = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} form suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == other_designation == === Nagano Prefectural other_designation === * '''other_designation''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | other_designation = Prefectural other_other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_other_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was other_designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | other_designation = Prefectural other_other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_other_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was other_designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == other_other_designation == === Nagano Prefectural other_other_designation === * '''other_other_designation''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_other_designation Description Board.. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial_chamber = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | other_designation = Prefectural other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was other_designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == other_designation == === Nagano Prefectural other_designation === * '''other_designation''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two other_designation Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | other_designation = Prefectural other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | marker = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was other_designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | other_designation = Prefectural other_designation (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is other_designation1 as a Nagano Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southother_designation Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was other_designation1 a Nagano Prefectural other_designation in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (other_designation Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=draft}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=draft}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=draft}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=draft}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=draft}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=draft}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=draft}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} -
