A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida Kofun Group|qq=}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato State|qq=}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai region|qq=}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Nagano Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iida|ja|飯田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū River|qq=}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida Kofun Group|qq=}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato State|qq=}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai region|qq=}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun group|lt=Iida Kofun Group|ja|飯田古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida Kofun Group|qq=}} featuring {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato State|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato State|qq=}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|ja|東海地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai region|qq=}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]]

Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:飯田市の歴史]]

A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}}

Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun in Nagano Prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coordinates|35|29|34.49|N|137|50|7.58|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}}

Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}}

The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}.
