Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q38279342]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - [[sn:commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q38279342]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q38279342}}

[[commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38279342]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]]. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun Group]] featuring [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] constructed under the influence of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato State]] in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38279342]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (代田山狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (left: rear section, right: front section) | alternate_name = | location = {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|WD=Q841129|lt=Iida}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.492914|N|137.835439|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Mound length: 42 m | built = Early 4th century | burial = Unknown | occupant = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Prefectural Historic Site (Nagano) | map = Japan Nagano | map_dot_label = Shirotayama
Kitsunezuka Kofun | omitted = 前方後方墳-南 | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (代田山狐塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|WD=Q841129|lt=Iida}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. The kofun is Historic Site1 as a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in the southern part of Nagano Prefecture in the {{ill|Shimoina region|ja|下伊那地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoina region|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this kofun was constructed at the eastern edge of a middle terrace along the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}}. Despite its age, the mound remains well-preserved, and a survey was conducted in 1993 (Heisei 5), though no excavation has yet taken place{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The mound has a {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type, with the front section facing southHistoric Site Description Board.. Due to its location on a steep slope, the eastern side, visible from lower ground, appears taller. A surrounding moat is said to have existed on the north and west sides of the mound, but no burial burial goods have been discovered{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, making it the oldest kofun in the {{ill|Ina Valley|ja|伊那谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ina Valley|qq=}} region{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. Unlike the later {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|WD=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun Group}} featuring {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} constructed under the influence of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato State}} in the late 5th century, this earlier kofun's {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} type suggests connections with forces from the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} rather than Yamato{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}. The kofun was Historic Site1 a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1994 (Heisei 6). == Mound Details == The mound's area are as follows{{Sfn|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}: * '''Mound length:''' 42 m (Historic Site Description Board states 44.5 m) * '''Rear section width:''' 28 m * '''Neck width:''' pp.3 m * '''Front section width:''' 13 m File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section mound peak File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from front section to rear section File:Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from rear section to front section == Historic Site == === Nagano Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun - Designated February 17, 1994 (Heisei 6)[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation).[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] (Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida").. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article composition}} * Historic Site Description Board (Nagano Prefectural Board of Education, Iida City Board of Education, 1997) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Kitsunezuka Kofun > Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun (Continued Ancient Mounds)|2002}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|References not used for article composition}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9003 |title=Survey of Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/8440 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Appearance and Development of Kofun in Iida - Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link from Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Comprehensive Database of Archaeological Site Reports in Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|WD=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun Group}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} * [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3893&seq=0 Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - The Eighty-Two Historic Site Foundation * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/kitsuneduka.html Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun] - Iida City Cultural Property Protection Intypeation Site "Bunka Iida" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|代田山狐塚古墳|version=95137433|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:代田山狐塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shirotayama Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q38279342}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]]
